title: Understanding Damping
text: Recall what a damping factor represents in the context of oscillations. What physical phenomenon does it model?

hintAnswer: It causes the magnitude to decrease.
title: Definition of Damping
text: Damping is typically associated with the gradual loss of energy in an oscillating system. What effect does this loss of energy have on the oscillation's magnitude?
choices: It causes the magnitude to decrease.
It causes the magnitude to increase.
It causes the frequency to change.
It causes the phase to shift.

title: Connecting Damping to Graph Features
text: How does the magnitude of an oscillation relate to its graph? Which characteristic of the graph is directly affected by the magnitude?

title: Identifying Graph Characteristics
text: In a sinusoidal graph, which parameter represents the maximum displacement or intensity of the oscillation?

title: Synthesizing the Effect
text: Combine your understanding of damping and graph characteristics. If damping causes the magnitude to decrease, what happens to the amplitude over time?

title: Describing the Overall Effect
text: If the amplitude decreases over time, how would you describe the overall shape of the graph of a damped harmonic motion function?
